Which U.S. Supreme Court case of 1931 established that the federal government could regulate interstate commerce, fundamentally expanding federal power during the New Deal era?
choices: NLRB v. Jones & Laughlin Steel Corp.
Schechter Poultry Corp. v. United States

questions.q: The Appalachian Trail, stretching over 2,190 miles from Georgia to Maine, was first completed as a continuous hiking path in which decade?
Which Caribbean island nation, formerly a British colony, declared independence in 1979 and was briefly led by the revolutionary Maurice Bishop before his assassination in 1983?
The Gaspé Peninsula, a significant geographic feature in eastern Canada, juts into which body of water that separates Quebec from Newfoundland and Labrador?
In 1881, the gunfight at the O.K. Corral in Tombstone, Arizona, was instigated by a dispute over the rustling of livestock; which outlaw clan was opposed by Wyatt Earp?
The Canadian Shield, one of North America's largest geographic regions, is primarily composed of which type of rock formed during the Precambrian era?
Which Jamaican political and cultural figure founded the Universal Negro Improvement Association in 1914 and promoted Pan-Africanism throughout the Caribbean and North America?
The Louisiana Purchase of 1803 doubled the size of the United States; which European power sold the territory to the U.S. to fund military campaigns?
Which pre-Columbian civilization, centered around the Valley of Mexico, constructed the pyramid complex at Tenochtitlan and dominated Mesoamerica until the Spanish conquest in the 16th century?
The Athabaska Oil Sands in Alberta, Canada, represent one of the world's largest proven reserves of which energy resource?
What were the Hawaiian Islands originally called by British explorers in the 1770s?
On what date does Mexico celebrate its Independence Day (often confused with Cinco de Mayo)?
Who was the last Aztec emperor, captured by the Spanish conquistador Hernán Cortés?

Moraine Lake, famed for its vivid turquoise color, is located in which national park?
Artist Frida Kahlo, known for vivid self-portraits, was from which country?
Tacos, now eaten worldwide, are a traditional food from which country?

Hawaii became the 50th U.S. state in which year?
The St. Lawrence River flows between Canada and which U.S. state?

Which mountain range runs along the western edge of North America and includes peaks like Mount Denali?
Toronto is the largest city in which Canadian province?
The Great Lakes are shared between the United States and Canada. How many Great Lakes are there in total?
Which U.S. state is the largest by land area?
New Orleans is strategically located at the mouth of which major river?
The Appalachian Mountains extend from which Canadian province southward to Georgia?
Which North American city served as the first capital of the United States?
The Death Valley region, found in which U.S. state, contains the lowest point in North America?
Which major city in Mexico is known as the country's cultural and artistic heart and was home to artists like Diego Rivera?
The Yucatan Peninsula, famous for Mayan ruins, is located in which country?
Vancouver is a major port city located on which coast of Canada?
Which U.S. state became part of the country in 1959 as the 50th state?
The Sierra Madre mountain range forms the backbone of which country's geography?
Montreal, Quebec's largest city, is situated on which river?
Which North American mountain range forms the western spine of the continent, stretching from British Columbia to New Mexico?
In what year did Mexico gain independence from Spain?
The Panama Canal connects which two bodies of water?
choices: The Caribbean Sea and the Pacific Ocean
The Atlantic Ocean and the Gulf of Mexico
The Pacific Ocean and Hudson Bay
The Atlantic Ocean and the Caribbean Sea
